prompt_text:
# Triggered Deceleration

## User Request
Create an OpenSCENARIO XML scenario where `ego` drives at a steady speed until a trigger fires, then decelerates smoothly to a slower target speed. Use a conservative trigger and keep the document minimal.

## Coverage Expectations
- One vehicle with clear init speed
- One trigger that starts the deceleration behavior
- A deceleration action that changes speed without adding unrelated maneuvers
- A bounded stop condition or explicit end state

retrieval_hits.title: SpeedAction
retrieval_hits.summary: Speed-control action. Both SpeedActionDynamics and SpeedActionTarget are required; the XSD uses xsd:all so child order is not constrained.
constraints: SpeedActionDynamics and SpeedActionTarget are both required.
strategy_summary: Keep required children present: SpeedActionDynamics, SpeedActionTarget.

retrieval_hits.title: SpeedActionDynamics
retrieval_hits.summary: Transition dynamics used by SpeedAction. This element name is an alias over the shared TransitionDynamics type.
strategy_summary: Resolve contextual variant before emitting this shared element name.

retrieval_hits.title: SpeedActionTarget
retrieval_hits.summary: Choice wrapper for absolute or relative speed targets.
constraints: Select exactly one of: RelativeTargetSpeed, AbsoluteTargetSpeed.
strategy_summary: Resolve contextual variant before emitting this shared element name.
Select exactly one branch from: RelativeTargetSpeed, AbsoluteTargetSpeed.

element_schema.description: Speed-control action. Both SpeedActionDynamics and SpeedActionTarget are required; the XSD uses xsd:all so child order is not constrained.
semantic_constraints: SpeedActionDynamics and SpeedActionTarget are both required.

draft_checklist: Keep required children present: SpeedActionDynamics, SpeedActionTarget.
Resolve contextual variant before emitting this shared element name.
Select exactly one branch from: RelativeTargetSpeed, AbsoluteTargetSpeed.
